notes: Spec asserts (1) the session appears in ready within ~1s of agent launch and (2) the PID is bound within ~1s. Gemini does NOT write its transcript at launch — it creates ~/.gemini/tmp/<project>/chats/session-<ts>-<id>.jsonl only after the first user message lands. So the daemon's process scanner discovers the gemini PID at the first tick (a proc-<PID> presession row bridges the gap), and the transcript-keyed session is born + settles to ready ~1s later once the first turn completes. Budgets anchor to recording start (agent launch).

text: PID bound within 1s of agent launch
Session appears in ready within ~1s (after the first turn, when Gemini writes the transcript)
